nodes.context: Indonesian organized crime groups, street level criminals to crime bosses.
Islamic organization that recruited local jago for thuggery against Chinese landlords.
Communist party that allied with Jakarta's underworld and jago leaders.
Nationalist movement that involved Jakarta's underworld in the late 1930s.
Japanese-sponsored military unit that challenged local gangs' power.
People's Brigade that worked with local jagos during the revolution.
Youth organization with personal links to the underworld.
Militia that collaborated with local jagos like Haji Darip.
Dutch auxiliary force that recruited local bosses.
Indonesian lasykar unit that some small units took allegiance to through SP88.
A unit of lasykar that engaged in psychological warfare against the Dutch.
Small unit of lasykar that took up characteristics of pre-war gangs.
Small unit of lasykar that took up characteristics of pre-war gangs.
Small unit of lasykar that took up characteristics of pre-war gangs.
Small unit of lasykar that took up characteristics of pre-war gangs.
A division whose dissolution led to disintegration of Jakarta's underworld.
Organization under which some lasykar members continued to security battalions under Dutch set up.
Nationalist and youth organization that many thugs and gangsters joined after petrus.
New breed preman organization organized along ethnic lines.
New breed preman organization organized along religious lines.

edges.evidence_quote: In the organization against the Chinese landlords and their agents, Sarekat Islam acquired its own access of thuggery through the recruitment of local jago.
edges.context: Sarekat Islam recruited local jago (preman) for thuggery.

edges.evidence_quote: Through their contacts with the workers and efforts to establish trade unions in Batavia, it brought the PKI into contact with Jakarta's underworld.
edges.context: PKI allied with Jakarta's underworld (preman) for organizational support.

edges.evidence_quote: By the late 1930s, the underworld was once again involved in the activities of the Gerindo.
edges.context: Gerindo involved Jakarta's underworld (preman) in its activities.

edges.evidence_quote: the underworld's position was challenged indirectly by the Japanese's construction of a rival institution in the form of Pembela Tanah Air (PETA).
edges.context: PETA challenged the power of local gangs (preman).

edges.evidence_quote: Through BARA (Barisan Rakyat, People's Brigade), they worked with the local jagos.
edges.context: BARA worked with local jagos (preman) during the revolution.

edges.evidence_quote: the Jakarta underworld were also a part of the API (Angkatan Pemuda Indonesia, Youth Generation of Indonesia) constituency.
edges.context: API had personal links to the Jakarta underworld (preman).

edges.evidence_quote: The formation of the LRJR (Lasykar Rakyat Jakarta Raya, People's Militia of Greater Jakarta) once again saw their collaboration with the local jagos like Haji Darip, Pak Macem and Kyai Haji Nurali.
edges.context: LRJR collaborated with local jagos (preman) like Haji Darip.

edges.evidence_quote: Local bosses such as Panji, Fakhruddin, Harun Umar and Sujono took up service under the Dutch auxiliary force.
edges.context: HAMOT recruited local bosses (preman) as auxiliary soldiers.

edges.evidence_quote: This saw small units of the lasykar who had taken some allegiance to the Bambu Runcing Division through the SP88 to take up the characteristics of pre-war gangs.
edges.context: SP88 was a unit that had allegiance to the Bambu Runcing Division.

edges.evidence_quote: These small units took up names such as Pasukan Siluman (Ghost Squad), Srigala Hitam (Black Jackal), Pemotong Leher (Neck Cutters) and Garuda Putih (White Eagle).
edges.context: Pasukan Siluman was one of the small units that took allegiance to SP88.

edges.evidence_quote: These small units took up names such as Pasukan Siluman (Ghost Squad), Srigala Hitam (Black Jackal), Pemotong Leher (Neck Cutters) and Garuda Putih (White Eagle).
edges.context: Srigala Hitam was one of the small units that took allegiance to SP88.

edges.evidence_quote: These small units took up names such as Pasukan Siluman (Ghost Squad), Srigala Hitam (Black Jackal), Pemotong Leher (Neck Cutters) and Garuda Putih (White Eagle).
edges.context: Pemotong Leher was one of the small units that took allegiance to SP88.

edges.evidence_quote: These small units took up names such as Pasukan Siluman (Ghost Squad), Srigala Hitam (Black Jackal), Pemotong Leher (Neck Cutters) and Garuda Putih (White Eagle).
edges.context: Garuda Putih was one of the small units that took allegiance to SP88.

edges.evidence_quote: organizations such as the Patriot Party (Pemuda Pancasila) continue to portray themselves as defenders of national unity
edges.context: Patriot Party is the political party formed by Pemuda Pancasila.
